channel.title: SpotPulse Trending
channel.description: Live web pulse — India & Global intelligence dashboard.

item.title: It's not just Iran and oil raising inflation. Prices also are reaccelerating in these other areas
item.description: Here's some of the places where prices are rising fast for consumers.

item.title: China’s Manufacturing Heartland Tested by Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Economic activity in China’s most important manufacturing hub is heaping pressure on power supplies, sparking an energy stress test as the Iran war chokes shipments of fuel.

item.title: Trinamool’s land policy delayed West Bengal border fencing: MHA’s 2023 affidavit in Supreme Court
item.description: On December 11, 2023, the Ministry of Home Affairs informed the Supreme Court that the West Bengal government had not adopted the LARR Act 2013, which allows land acquisition for public purposes

item.title: CommScope Sued by Lenders for at Least $150 Million Over Alleged Breach
item.description: CommScope Holding Co. was sued by a group of lenders who allege the telecommunications provider breached a debt agreement by failing to pay a prescribed premium of at least $150 million following a key asset sale.

item.title: S&P 500 & NASDAQ Fall as Oil Jumps | Closing Bell
item.description: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Google thwarts hacker group’s AI-driven mass exploitation plan - Crypto Briefing
item.description: Google thwarts hacker group’s AI-driven mass exploitation plan Crypto Briefing

item.title: Tiger Woods' DUI case: Florida prosecutors to get golfer's prescription drug records
item.description: A Florida judge on Tuesday ruled that prosecutors can get access to Tiger Woods’ prescription drug records in his DUI case. Woods faces charges after a March crash in Jupiter involving alleged speeding, impairment signs, and refusal of a urine test.

item.title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled highlights: CBI takes custody of man detained by Nashik Police
item.description: The schedule for the re-examination would be announced within the “next seven to 10 days”, according to the DG of NTA, Abhishek Singh

item.title: What Trump and Xi Want From Their Summit
item.description: The leaders are expected to talk trade, Taiwan, and the Iran war.

item.title: Bond Bears Reload Fed Rate Hike Wagers on Stubborn Inflation
item.description: Traders have renewed bearish bets on US Treasuries, lifting expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates as oil prices and inflation push higher.

item.title: Trump Says US Inflation Is 'Short Term'
item.description: President Donald Trump says inflation in the US is only "short term." He spoke before leaving for Beijing on Tuesday. US inflation accelerated in April due to rising gasoline and grocery costs, exceeding wage growth. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: World News Today Live Updates on May 13, 2026 : Cannes 2026 opens with Peter Jackson tribute, Jane Fonda’s remarks and James Franco return on Day 1
item.description: World News Today Live Updates on May 13, 2026: Stay informed on global events with our in-depth world news coverage, bringing you the latest developments across politics, economy, and culture. Discover insights into international affairs, breaking news, and the trends shaping our interconnected world, all in one place.

item.title: Meta US Employees Organise Protest Against Mouse Tracking Tech
item.description: The flyers encourage staffers to sign an online petition against the move, saying "workers are legally protected when they choose to organize for the improvement of working conditions."

item.title: American Electric Power Utility Offers $2.6 Billion in Stock
item.description: American Electric Power Co., one of the biggest utilities in the US, is seeking to raise $2.6 billion in a share sale as artificial intelligence technology drives booming demand for electricity.

item.title: Inflation Jumps as War-Driven Energy Costs Drive Up Prices - The New York Times
item.description: Inflation Jumps as War-Driven Energy Costs Drive Up Prices The New York Times

item.title: UK fines water supplier $1.3M for exposing data of 664k customers
item.description: The Information Commissioner's Office has fined South Staffordshire Water Plc and parent company South Staffordshire Plc £963,900 ($1.3 million) over a cyberattack that exposed the personal data of 663,887 customers and employees. [...]

item.title: Exclusive: Satellite Images Confirm Pak May Be Shielding Iran's Military Aircraft
item.description: Iranian aircraft parked at Pakistan's Nur Khan airbase High-res here

item.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq end lower as inflation, Iran tensions weigh
item.description: US stocks closed lower as hotter-than-expected inflation and escalating US-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq retreated from record highs, led by tech weakness, while Dow held steady. Rising oil prices and fears of prolonged inflation reduced hopes of Fed rate cuts and lifted rate-hike bets.

item.title: Baltimore ship crash: U.S. slaps criminal cases against Singapore, Chennai firms and Indian national
item.description: They have been charged with conspiracy, wilfully failing to immediately inform U.S. Coast Guard of a known hazardous condition, obstruction of an agency proceeding, and false statements

item.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

item.title: Introspect on reputational cost of aiding terror exporter Pakistan, India tells China
item.description: India's MEA urged China to consider the reputational damage of backing Pakistan's terrorist infrastructure, following reports of Chinese tech assistance during last year's Operation Sindoor. The ministry stated these reports corroborated existing knowledge, emphasizing that supporting such infrastructure impacts a nation's standing.

item.title: Breaking: Mumbai-based Sarvodaya Cooperative Bank licence cancelled by RBI - MSN
item.description: Breaking: Mumbai-based Sarvodaya Cooperative Bank licence cancelled by RBI MSN

item.title: A society at large under question: rebuild a culture where safety of children is collective responsibility
item.description: The sexual assault and murder of a minor girl in Maharashtra has once again exposed the disturbing vulnerabilities that continue to confront children in our society

item.title: Traders will soon be able to bet on computer chip prices as AI drives costs skyward
item.description: Contracts can be used to hedge against rising GPU rental rates and other operational costs

item.title: High Blood Sugar Levels? Here's What You Should Do To Maintain Your Heart Health
item.description: When blood sugar remains high for a long time, it can damage blood vessels and this increases the risk of high blood pressure, blocked arteries, heart attacks, and strokes.

item.title: Signal adds security warnings for social engineering, phishing attacks
item.description: Signal has introduced new in-app confirmations and warning messages as additional safeguards against phishing and social engineering attempts that could lead to various forms of fraud. [...]

item.title: Israel is worried that Trump will strike a ‘bad deal’ with Iran, leaving war objectives unmet - CNN
item.description: Israel is worried that Trump will strike a ‘bad deal’ with Iran, leaving war objectives unmet CNN

item.title: Ukraine hits Russia’s distant gas facilities after Moscow’s attacks kill 6
item.description: The attack comes in retaliation for the Russian attacks that killed at least six in Ukraine, according to the president.

item.title: Farooq joins liquor debate, says even Sheikh Abdullah didn’t favour prohibition; Mirwaiz, BJP seek ban
item.description: National Conference president Farooq Abdullah weighed in on a liquor ban debate in Jammu and Kashmir, citing his father's 1977 stance that revenue generation was key. He argued that prohibition wouldn't stop consumption, as people would source alcohol from outside. Abdullah also questioned critics' silence when liquor shops previously opened across the region.

item.title: Samsung Electronics, labour union fail to reach pay deal as strike looms
item.description: Samsung Electronics and its South Korean labor union have not reached a pay agreement. This impasse follows marathon talks. A lengthy strike is now planned for May 21. The union representative expressed regret that union requests were not addressed. This potential strike could disrupt the production of AI and other chips.

item.title: Terrorist killed, Poonch infiltration bid foiled
item.description: Army troops foiled an infiltration attempt in Poonch's Krishna Ghati sector on Tuesday, neutralizing one terrorist approximately 300 meters inside the Indian side of the Line of Control. This incident follows several other thwarted infiltration bids in the Jammu region in recent months, highlighting ongoing vigilance along the LoC.

item.title: Asia Isn’t as Peaceful as It Seems
item.description: Trouble is brewing as norms against war dissolve.

item.title: Retail inflation up a tad on rise in food, bullion prices
item.description: Retail inflation reached a four-month peak of 3.5% in April. This rise was fueled by increasing food and gold/silver prices. Economists anticipate further inflationary pressures ahead. The Reserve Bank of India is expected to maintain its current policy rate. Inflation is nearing the RBI's target, suggesting a pause in rate hikes for now.

item.title: CEA Nageswaran says West Asia crisis a balance of payments stress test; to impact CAD, inflation & exchange rate
item.description: Large companies must pay micro and small enterprises on time. This eases their working capital and reduces funding costs. Managing the current account, financing it, and preventing currency depreciation are key macroeconomic goals for FY27. The West Asia crisis presents a balance of payments stress test.

item.title: Replit Showcases AI-Driven Software Development Themes at SaaStr Annual 2026 - TipRanks
item.description: Replit Showcases AI-Driven Software Development Themes at SaaStr Annual 2026 TipRanks

item.title: US inflation jumps to 3.8% as energy costs surge from Iran war
item.description: The key measure of US inflation rises its highest level since May 2023 as consumers feel the impact of the Iran war.

item.title: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed governor, clears way for chair vote
item.description: The upper chamber voted to approve Warsh's nomination by a 51-45 vote, on a mostly party-line basis.

item.title: BREAKING: U.S. President Donald Trump Makes Important Statements – “The Ukraine-Russia War Will End Soon” - Cryptonews.net
item.description: BREAKING: U.S. President Donald Trump Makes Important Statements – “The Ukraine-Russia War Will End Soon” Cryptonews.net

item.title: Breaking News: U.S. inflation shot up to a 3.8% annual rate in April, as the war in Iran raised energy prices and costs across the country. - facebook.com
item.description: Breaking News: U.S. inflation shot up to a 3.8% annual rate in April, as the war in Iran raised energy prices and costs across the country. facebook.com

item.title: Fortinet warns of critical RCE flaws in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator
item.description: Fortinet has released security patches for two critical vulnerabilities in FortiSandbox and FortiAuthenticator that could enable attackers to run commands or arbitrary code. [...]
